post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 214: Chaos at the Masquerade

If you are not currently alive in this game, please do not post in this thread. Visit this thread to sign up for future games.
Credits to Binomial-theorem/chaotic_iak/willwang123 (among others) for the rule template. Some changes have been made to the ruleset; please read all the rules.

Story
"Just go," they said. "It'll be fun," they said.

Oh boy, do I wish I hadn't listened.

The Mafia Masquerade, as it was called, was a gathering of around a hundred Mafia aficionados from around the region. It was marketed as an old-fashioned ball, but really, it was just a large party. They talked strategy, played some games, danced, fun times. But the masquerade in its name did have to come from somewhere--everybody had to show up dressed up as their favorite power role, town or scum.

So, I showed up. It's not important what I dressed up as, the costume wasn't even great anyways. But the guy at the front saw it, deemed it legit, and waved me in.

========================================

The rush of noise overwhelmed me at first. People were talking, and they were talking a lot. I hadn't gone to a gathering like this since corona hit, so it was a lot to take in at first.

"Oh, it's a cop! Ya know, there was one game I played in which..."
"TFPTSLBOOIA, hah. Nice reference."
"Serial Killer? Who in the world likes playing Serial Killer?"
Nobody was talking to me. That was fine by me, anyways. I stood awkwardly in the corner of the room, watching the ballroom as people talked, laughed, and generally enjoyed themselves. It was fine, I guess.

Then the lights went out.

I figured this would be part of the game. Suddenly we'd find one of the hosts "dead" on the ground, set everyone up with their roles, and the game would begin.
The murmuring told me otherwise. "Can you get the emergency generator on?" "The power in the convention center isn't down, the lights are just out." "Did somebody cut them?"

A voice cut through the whispers.
"Good. Now that I have your attention..."
A single spotlight turned on, showing a person wearing a classical masquerade mask in the center of the room. They smiled.
"Who are you? What did you do?"
"Why, I'm the Moderator! And we're going to be playing a game of Mafia!"
I frowned. Really?
"Isn't that what we always do here?" Somebody said. They were close to the masked person, so they were visible in the spotlight. Dressed up as a Doctor. No, maybe a CPR Doctor? It was hard to tell in that light.
The masked person smiled. "Well, of course. But your games are simply so bland. When you get voted out or nightkilled, you just leave the room and spectate for the rest of the game. Boooooringggggggg! So I decided to help make your game a little more realistic. Do you get what I mean?"

Silence. There's no way they--

"Anyways, let's begin!"


Rules
  • Read the rules. Including this one.
  • Follow all the rules. Including this one.
  • Do not post at night.
  • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification. Questions asked in-thread will only be answered in-PM, if at all, unless I specifically believe it is something worth telling the general public.
  • Replacement requests and the like should be sent privately.
  • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the moderators, unless you are specifically allowed to do so. Do not discuss other ongoing games in this thread.
  • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
  • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
  • Do not pretend to edit or otherwise pretend to break a rule.
  • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
  • Do not edit the tags on the thread. This also applies if you are not playing the game.
  • Do not flavorclaim. Flavorclaiming in this game is grounds for a modkill.
  • To confirm, respond to your Role PM with your alignment and the one special rule in this game. Please don't post until you've confirmed.


Voting/Hanging
  • This game is based on deadline hanging. The player with the most votes at the time the deadline is reached is hanged. Ties will be explained in the next section. Starting Day 2, majority hanging is enabled, which means day will end early if at any time a player has more than half the amount of total votes on them. If majority hang is not reached, deadline hanging occurs.
  • If there is a tie vote at the end of the day between two players, the result of it will be randomized. However, if there are an equal number of votes for No Hang and a player, No Hang will be the result.
  • Post votes in code styling in the format [code]Vote: <insert player here>[/code]. For example, "[code]Vote: summitwei[/code]" is an acceptable vote.
  • Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames. Capitalization should be correct.
  • Improperly made votes (including votes in hide tags or any of the unacceptable forms above) will not be counted under any circumstances. I will not tell you if your vote is invalid. There is one exception to this - see below.
  • The characters "I" (capital i) and "l" (lowercase L) are considered the same character for the purpose of voting. That is, attempting to fakevote by substituting one of these characters in will not work. Most other fakevotes are acceptable, but please PM me to confirm that it is. If we do not notice that you fakevoted, it may be counted as a real vote, so it is a good idea to PM me every time you intend to fakevote.
  • You may vote for no hanging to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post [code]Vote: No Hang[/code]. (Note: This is not the same thing as unvoting.)
  • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, but you can. Unvote in the format "[code]Unvote[/code]". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
  • Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day more than half of the players currently alive are voting for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
  • Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day at least half of the players alive are voting to not hang, then nobody will be hanged. It will then be twilight.
  • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
  • During twilight (the time between the end of deadline and the moderator's "do not post" post), players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
  • Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.


Activity
  • Each day will be 144 hours (6 days). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
  • The deadline will occur at the start of the minute listed. For example, if the deadline is 7:00 PM, then 6:59:59 is before the deadline and 7:00:00 is after.
  • Each night will be 24 real-life hours long.
  • Actions are due 1 hour before their respective deadlines. This is to allow me to process them and start the next phase without delay.
  • When the action deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive an action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night. (In general, actions may be proxyed between partners.)
  • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night and start of the next day.
  • Activity rule for this game: While not on V/LA, you are expected to make at least one post between every two activity checks, which will be done roughly every 48 hours. If you miss this, you may be replaced without warning. Do not try to abuse this by saying "activity post" every two days - I will warn or replace you if I feel you are posting purely to bypass this requirement.
  • If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long. If your V/LA will be longer than 2-3 days, please either try to find a way to participate in the game during that time or ask for a replacement.


Etiquette
  • Do not use inappropriate language.
  • Do not make any personal attacks.
  • Be mature.
  • Do not angleshoot. This is a rather vague rule that is subject to moderator judgment, but angleshooting basically involves using outside-game information or moderator posts or the like to attempt to make deductions about players' roles. Don't do this.
  • Do not reference communication from the moderator. Certainly, do not quote communication from the moderator. This includes Role PMs.
  • For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to two warnings, and SE’s/IC's up to one. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force-replaced, depending on the scenario. Please do not make me modkill you.


Moderators
  • I (summitwei) am the Moderator of Game 214.
  • Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators.
  • Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
  • Large bolded text (size 200-250) may be used for other types of announcements.
  • Please do not do things that have the effect of impersonating a moderator. For the most part, though, posting in colors, etc. is fine.
  • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.


Setup Tab
This game is a closed 13-player game. It is Semi-BM. There may be hidden mechanics.

The most important particular rule in this game is to not flavorclaim. Flavorclaiming is grounds for a modkill.


Alive (2)
  1. BobDBuilder321 (Cat Wehr, Mafia, won at endgame)
  2. Mathletesv (Teodor Tintomec, Town, lost at endgame)


Dead (9)
Osmium_ (Carol O'Leer, town) was hanged Day 1
snoozingnewt (Clint de Chinon, town) died Night 1
mark888 (Jake Pelier, town) lost the thunderdome Day 2
awesomeming327. (Brad Goudy, town) was hanged Day 2
kevinmathz (Bud Rivers, mafia) died Night 2
tikkitamatoa (Butler Voode, town) died Night 2
etr217 (Dora Deeps, town) was hanged Day 3
VKU427 (Leo Crop, mafia) died Night 3
cai40 (Professor Bob Lee Tai Johnson, town) was hanged Day 4
Madelyn (Olga Taird, town) died Night 4
Fvr_Vain (Loki, 3rd party) died Night 4


Significant Events
Day 1 begins
Madelyn replaces Florinaapplezhu
Day 1 ends
Night 1 ends; Day 2 begins
Thunderdome begins between Madelyn and mark888
Thunderdome ends
Day 2 ends
Night 2 ends; Day 3 begins
Day 3 ends
Night 3 ends
Day 4 ends
Night 4 ends
Game ends -- Mafia victory


Warnings
awesomeming327. - OOT
Mathletesv - OOT
BobDBuilder321 - Referencing mod communication
cai40 - Semi-flavorclaiming
cai40 - Discussing flavor-related information
BobDBuilder321 - Referencing mod communication


Do not post until this line is crossed out.

The game has ended. The Mafia have won.

post_rendered:
Story
The lights went out again, but the voices began picking up. People were rapidly discussing what in the world was happening. Some shouts. "Are they gonna kill us all?"
When I realized that there were lights again, I found myself in a smaller room. 12 more people standing around, just as confused as I was.
"Who are you all? How'd I get here?" It wasn't me saying it, but he might as well have been reading my mind.
"I--we're all in the same boat. That psychopath..."
We stood there for a solid minute.
"Then, what should we do?"
"Isn't it obvious? We play."


Day 1 has begun. The deadline for Day 1 is 7:10 PM EDT on 9/16/20.

post_rendered:
Story
We spent a good amount of time, maybe half an hour, trying to figure out how to escape. Nobody wanted to play this game. But the exit door to the room was locked from the outside. None of the other ways to exit seemed to work, either.
We were in a bedroom. There were probably once windows, but they had been barred over with solid metal sheets. Some guy--muscular, he said his name was Brad or something--tried to pry them off, but it was no use. I took a look at the vent, since I once saw a visual novel in which some guy escaped his prison by crawling out of a vent, but they were too small. We were really stuck, and the only way out was... to play.
"How are we supposed to play when we don't even have roles? Usually when we play, we're sent our roles on our phones. But well..." She took out her phone. It was dead. A few of the others knew this too, apparently, though I didn't think of checking until I pulled out my phone and realized that my 95% had dropped down to 0% in the span of an hour.
So much for calling for help.

We sat there pondering it for a bit. Then some guy had an idea. "Wait. You all came... dressed up as roles right? And... some of you are Mafia roles."
"Uh huh..."
"So, there are your roles. Easy."

Well, if there was one way to shut people up, that would work. I looked around the room, quickly gauging for reactions. Every face still as a stone. They've clearly been training their poker faces for this.
"Isn't it obvious what to do here, then? Let's just have everyone say their roles. If someone's an arsonist or something, we kill them." Some guy spoke up. His voice was tinged with humor, as if he was half laughing while saying it. Apparently he was the only person who found our situation remotely humorous.
"You're going to kill another person just because of what they wore?"
"...Good point."

With nothing better to do, we started to introduce ourselves. Brad went first. He worked as a security guard at some complex. He grinned as he pointed to his cosplay. "Fitting, huh?" Some woman went next. Got up, stood in the center of the room. "Hi, I'm Carol, and-"
"You have one minute left to decide on a hang for Day 1."
Wait, what?
The room went from quiet to chaos. "What are we supposed to do?"
"Calm down, everyone. I say we No Hang today. We're not killing anyone. Maybe we can Happily Ever After this situation." Someone was at least *trying* to take control of the situation. Even if his idea was about to get dunked on.
"Oh yeah, you're not allowed to No Hang on Day 1."
"...@#$%!"
"Five... Four..."
People started pointing fingers. Doesn't matter who it is, as long as it isn't me.
"Three... Two... One..."
I wildly chose someone, too. No time to think. Just do.
"TIME!"
I took a quick glance around. Over half of the players had their fingers on Carol. Center of attention, and all that. She looked around, eyes widening. "Hold on, I-"

Suddenly she stopped speaking. Grabbed her throat. Fell to her knees. Somebody probably could have tried to do something, but everyone was too busy staring for that.
She collapsed face-first into the floor. People started moving again. Brad ran up to her and felt her neck. "She's dead. No pulse."
This is real. This is real, and we're all going to die.
"Alright, everyone. Go to sleep. Close your eyes. You'll be woken up to do your night actions if you have any. You know the drill. We'll have someone come around and collect the body."
I felt my eyelids droop. I tried to shake my head awake, but it wasn't responding. My hands felt numb. No, I-- don't-- want-- to-- sleep--

As I lay down on the ground, my quest utterly defeated, I only had one thought.
They're toying with us.


It was a tie. Osmium_, Carol O'Leer, was hanged at random.

It is now night. Do not post. Night ends at 7:30 PM ET on 9/17/20.

post_rendered:
Story
I don't know when I woke up. Had it been ten minutes? An hour? A day? I certainly didn't feel rested at all. The others seemed just as woozy.
I glanced at the center of the room, where Carol's body had been before my nap. It had disappeared in the interim. Had the door been opened from the outside? Most likely. But if they were doing... this, when the doors opened, we surely couldn't get out that way.

A bloodcurdling scream shook me out of my thoughts. I got on my feet and ran to the source of the scream. Please no, don't let it be a--
There was a corpse lying on the ground. A knife through the chest. It was the guy who came up with the idea for everyone to claim their roles before Carol died. He was just a teenager, though his clothes seemed indicative of someone even younger.
I turned away from the sight. I've never been particularly able to stomach the sight of blood, and the sight of the dead kid there made me almost want to puke.
"There's a note. Next to the body." A middle-aged man pointed to the paper. He picked up the note and read it aloud.
"We can't help it. They forced us to kill someone. We're truly sorry. May the best faction win."
I took another quick glance around. To gauge for reactions. But all I saw were looks of determination.

So, the game has begun.


snoozingnewt, Clint de Chinon, died Night 1.

It is now day. You may post. Day 2 ends at 7:30 PM ET on 6/23/20.

Majority Hang is disabled in a thunderdome.

Story
"Don't think I've never seen this type of behavior before. You're a wolf!"
"Oh come on, this is just OMGUS. I think you're the Mafia here."
You could listen to the ongoing argument and think it was just a normal game of Mafia. One without lives on the line. One without people who were already dead.
I always tried staying out of these kinds of arguments, and this time, it was no exception. They began yelling at each other, and more people began joining in. I tried to calm people down, but it was no use.
Finally, the instigator of the argument held up her hand for silence. "There's only one way to settle this. I challenge you to a duel!"

And we were no longer in that room. Suddenly I found myself in the viewing stands of a large Roman colosseum. The two duelists were in the arena, holding swords, staring each other down.
Then the challenged player yelled. "Have at thee!" He charged, raising his sword. The other smiled, swinging her sword up to deflect the attack. Then the fight began. They traded blow after blow, neither of them actually able to touch the other. It went on for a few minutes, perfectly evenly matched. I almost missed the moment when the man attempted to deflect an attack, but it was a fake-out, and she delivered a clean cut to his side.
The battle didn't last for much longer after that. Slowly she continued wearing him down until eventually he fell to one knee. "I... surrender. Just... don't kill me..."
"Nah, that's good enough for me." She said, sheathing her sword.
"Oh, come on! You're really going to have to make me do all of the work?"
It was the masked person's voice. Where was it coming from? What did they mean? I got my answer shortly when a spear flew out of seemingly nowhere, piercing him cleanly through. The light went out of his eyes instantly.
Another dead. Still no answers.

I realized after another moment that I was back in the bedroom again. Jake's body didn't come with us, and at some point Clint's body had been removed from the room as well. Three dead.
Let us hope the same fate does not befall us all.


mark888, Jake Pelier, has lost the thunderdome.

Day continues. Day 2 still ends at 7:30 PM EST on 6/23/20.

Ok 1/2 of the reads (ok maybe less) on ppl suck imo so I'm going to shove my opinions down your throats.

Hopefully this tastes good
cai: towny, on a scale of 1-10: 3. Cai hasn't done anything rly so far, which is bad, but I feel like it's NAI and just has to do with competence. I tr cai because he seems to want to help scumhunt, but doesn't know how, so he asks a lot of questions. What he's doing feels genuine, but not ready to tr him too much just off of tone and asking questions.

vain: towny, on a scale of 1-10: 4. I tr vain cuz he's making a lot of valid points (not very towny by itself), his reactions are towny (like when kevin kept attacking him for horrible reasons), and I just agree with him on most things. Scum could be faking though (but then I must just be rly bad at this), but not seeing it. THe possibility still exists though, and theres a decentish chance, so vain is only 5.

VKU: towny, on a scale of 1-10: 1. Now let me be clear, none of VKU's actions have been towny or scummy. However, I tr him because ppl are bwing on him with horrible reasons (well its not exactly bwing because ppl have reasons... that are bad?) and a lot of the time there is scum on bws. However, I'm not reading him too much off this, because I'm pretty sure you can still sr scum for bad reasons? | Now for why I find the reason that ppl are voting him bad: vku voted osmium at deadline. Obviously because he didn't want to die. Now both town and scum don't want to die. The fact that vku sred osmium at the beginning of D1 makes me think vkus vote had more to it then just being an act of self-preservation. However, before this matter is settled, I want vku's explanation cuz I dont like how he hasnt been defending himself. Except, wouldn't scum defend themselves? or maybe vku just doesnt understand the argument against them so they're not sure how to defend. hmmm.

ming: null. I said I was being ess lenient, so I am. most of ming's posts are good and sound towny, but they could be faked by scum. end of story. i also didnt like post 597. but i also dont rly like the ming wagon, but at least the reasons are slightly better than the vku wagon.

mathlete: null. Posts are good ig (mostly valid points), didn't rly get anything out of it. will look at him more later so I can actually get a read on him.

tikki: null. Posts are good, I disagree with their opinions. I need to also look at them more.

etr: null. Posts pinged me abit, but I need to look at them more.

Madelyn: sr, on a scale on 1-10: 3. They've done some good things and bad things. Them saying stuff in spanish is just weird on 2nd thougt. At first I thought it was towny, but not it's just NAI, but it's just weird. Like 0 scum or town motivation in it. Madelyn's posts seem ok. What I rly didn't like was the whole thunderdome thing and how they only claimed they started it after the flavor made it obvious, which seems pretty scummy to me. Some people argue Madelny is conftown because they posted how to figure out ur roles, but I think it's NAI. There's both town and scum motivation. Town: um helps others figure out their role. Scum: were being heavily attacked, so did it to get free towncred because town would figure it out by D4 anyways (night actions and town just realizing it in general). So that's NAI.

kevin: sr, on a scale of 1-10: 5. Most of kevin's posts have been bad. some of his posts were good (and easily fakable), but his posts defending himself against vain were bull. complete bull. like just scummy. The only reason I'm only 5/10 sure is because TBTBAW. sure thats WIFOM, but i rly cant imagine kevin doing this as scum. but its even harder to see him doing it as town. waitttt. Hot Take: kevin's 3p. hmmm


for some reason that went from town to scum, but it was actually just the playerlist backwards.

hmmm

I'll vote kevin a bit close to deadline.

so i can hear other's opinions.

post_rendered:
Story
Nobody could really speak after that happened. It was gruesome. But we had to move on. There were Mafia, to kill, after all.
I received my first accusation that day, too.
"Hey, you. You've been quiet. Almost... too quiet."

To be fair, this was true. I don't like talking. I much rather prefer watching the game unfold, taking in as much data as I can, and then rooting out the scum. I tried to explain this, but the accuser wasn't buying it.
"Well, surely by now you have some thoughts. Who do you think it is?"

"Him." And I pointed to Brad. He had been discussing with someone else quietly in the corner, and didn't see my accusation.
"Huh? Why him?"
"His mannerisms. He's supposed to be the strong, silent type, right? Why's he so nervous about everything?"
"You really think he's nervous? I haven't really gotten that feeling."
"Oh, I just know."
"Eh. Good enough for me." He nodded, and stood up. "Guys, I think I know one of the Mafia."

He laid out an intricate case, much more detailed than my gut feeling. Where in the world did he come up with that stuff? I watched as Brad's face grew more panicked as the case unfolded.
"Uh... I'm not scum."
"Yeah? Why should I believe you?"
Another voice joined the fray. "Surely you have a better defense than this."
"I-"
"You *knew* when the deadline was on the first day, right? You left poor Carol there to die!"
"What? Of course I didn't! I didn't even vote her at the end!"
But his defenses were no use. Nobody believed him. When the day ended, we had 9 out of 10 votes on Brad.

"Day has ended."
Meanwhile, Brad was still pleading with everyone. "If I'm scum, they're not going to bus me like this! Seriously! There's still time to-"
His face went blank, his movements no longer his own. Slowly, he walked over to the window that was covered by a steel plate. In a single motion, he took the plate and ripped it clean off.
I looked outside. The ground was barely in sight. I knew what he was about to do, but I couldn't move. Couldn't do a thing about it.

He climbed out of the window and fell, not a noise escaping his lips.


awesomeming327., Brad Goudy, was hanged.

It is now Night 2. Do not post. Night 2 ends on 9/24/20 at 7:35 PM ET.

post_rendered:
Story
I wanted to speak some more, but I felt quite drowsy, and knew this would be a repeat of the previous night. I managed to drag myself to the bed before losing consciousness.

I dreamt that "night". It was Brad, again. He stood there in front of the window, a fall that would kill anyone. He was facing away from it, though. Pleading. "Don't kill me! I haven't done anything! I'm just a townie!"
I tried to stop myself, but I couldn't. I pushed him, and he fell out of the window. Screaming all the way.

That was when I woke up. I didn't need a psychologist to figure out what that dream meant. I killed him. If it weren't for me, Brad would still be alive.

As I started to calm down and recover from the dream, I noticed a body near me that I could swear wasn't near me before. It was the guy that discussed with me yesterday, the one that built the case on Brad.
"Hey, wake up."
But he wouldn't budge. Maybe just really sleepy.
I looked around some more. One, two, three... eight people total, counting me. Wait, shouldn't there be nine?

Quickly I roused everyone else awake. The guy near me was still asleep, but everyone else woke up. "What's the matter?"
"Someone's missing. Who is it?"
Someone responded. "I know who you're talking about, but I don't think he ever gave his name." He then proceeded to give a description, but was interrupted by another scream. I turned around and saw someone knelt over the asleep guy, rolled over, revealing a welt.
"Dead. Blunt force trauma, I think. Not an expert though."

The seven of us looked around again. I nodded. "And a new day begins."


kevinmathz, Bud Rivers, died Night 2.

- setup is probably 10-3. but the three scum roles are probably super strong - on the same power of bus driver probably given how many useful town PRs there were. 9-4 doesn’t really make any sense.
- Some investigative role probably still exist given none flipped.
- vig exist for obvious reasons.
- sk probably doesn’t exist because there was no extra night 1 kill.
- BM mechanic probably just refers to not giving exact role to players but just a anagramed name.
- language thing was troll and should not be read into. (Madelyn can still be very much scum)
- protective roles are probably all dead so don’t claim because 3 protective roles makes absolutely no sense. (Bodyguard and jailkeeper already all flipped)

A Rant? or Motivational Speech? or a big ol pile of dung?
To good to be true implies that you feel like something is wrong with whatever is 'too good'.

Which in this case is that there must be something wrong with the fact we have 3 conftowns off 1 flip.

That implies you think that there might have been bussing going on.

I can see where you come from, and can't deny it, because the possibility does exist. However, is that likely? Does it seem like bussing? Have anything indicating bussing? Analyze the interactions closely, and ask yourself those questions. When you're done, I would like it if you could report your findings, and preferably have a solid opinion on if there's bussing or not.

Maybe there is bussing. I have looked at the interactions in detail, and formed my opinion, there is no bussing going on. If you would like, I can share why I think there is no bussing and why (in detail).

You cite you're uneasy because of 3 conftowns from 1 flip. Sure, that's perfectly fine. First, I would like you to see it from my POV. I already know I'm town, so kevin's flip actually only made 2 people conftowns. Perhaps this is why I'm more willing to believe, there is a difference of 1 conftown (and believe me, that can be game-changing). Or maybe I'm just biased here, willing to believe that vain and vku are conftown because I'm lumped in with them in terms of interactions with kevin (although I've been trying to avoid confbias).

Anways, if you find that you cannot decipher the interactions, it's OK. I've noticed that we've all been so preoccupied with interactions that we're not reading off other things that we normally would. So go ahead, read off other things than interactions (which we should all be doing actually), and solve the game that way (unless you're scum).

Being uneasy about the interactions is perfectly justified. So go figure them out once and for all, or ignore them and solve the game without interactions. Even better, do both!

post_rendered:
um cai

so you have a flavorname.

your flavorname is the text in your role pm which is highlighted in a color.

1. what color is it highlighted in?
2. that is an anagram of your role (like 1 shot bp in 209). so you have to unscramble it to figure out what your role is.

so go here: https://wiki.mafiascum.net/index.php?title=Portal:Roles

and look for possible roles that have the same letters as your flavorname.

you can look for roles by clicking the links next to "By type"

the links should say alignment-changing, communicative, deceptive and so on

click on all of those until you find a role that has the exact same letters are your flavorname

that is your role

post_rendered:
Story
Another day. It was quite heated. Seeing Brad's death reminded everyone that nobody here wanted to die.
Me? I couldn't bring up an attack. I didn't want to openly be responsible for another death. So I sat back and watched.

Accusations flew. Nobody even knew of my role in the previous hang. If they did, I probably would have gotten voted out for it. Instead I was questioned about my lack of doing stuff in general.
"You've been under the radar this whole game. That's obviously the sign of a wolf."
"I've been trying to solve. I need more data."
"Oh yeah? What's your solve, then?" She was all up in my face.
"Didn't I just say I needed more data?"
"Hmph."
I heard another whisper. "Doesn't she seem... awfully aggressive?"
"Yeah, I agree. She's probably a wolf trying to push the LHF." The whisper back said.
"Okay, let's vote her, then."
I latched on. I hated attacking like this, but I needed to live. "I think you're scum. What's your role?"
"You think I'm scum? I'm a freaking dayvig! I can confirm it by shooting you right now, if you'd like."
Another whisper. "Mafia Suicide Bomber."
"Let's maj her before she gets the chance!"

So we did. It was her or me, after all. And it sure as heck couldn't be me.


etr217, Dora Deeps, has been hanged.

It is now Night 3. Do not post. Night 3 ends on 9/28/20 at 2:40 PM ET.

post_rendered:
some mod errors and stuff
madelyn's role was intended to be 1-shot, which probably should have been included in the flavor name. oops.

some time between d1 and d2, i said some stuff in discord that could have been interpreted as modspew of awesomeming327. town. seems it didn't affect the game too much, but putting it here anyways.


setup thoughts
i still like the mechanic of the game

vain's role was probably too strong; in retrospect i think i make it a "choose two" or something of the four modifiers (although 1-shot bp and strongman are obviously the strongest so idk).

expected the mechanic to be gotten at d3 and not d2; not sure how significantly this affects everything, but yeah


game thoughts
overall i think both sk and mafia played quite well. vain did some nice wolfhunting; mafia set up some great not w/w interactions that carried their backs.
town played pretty badly imo. quickhammers on d3 and d4 were kind of dumb and town should never have let them happen. and eod1 was a tragedy. make a wagon at eod! it's not hard!

[hide=some mod errors and stuff]madelyn's role was intended to be 1-shot, which probably should have been included in the flavor name. oops.

some time between d1 and d2, i said some stuff in discord that could have been interpreted as modspew of awesomeming327. town. seems it didn't affect the game too much, but putting it here anyways.[/hide]

[hide=setup thoughts]i still like the mechanic of the game

vain's role was probably too strong; in retrospect i think i make it a "choose two" or something of the four modifiers (although 1-shot bp and strongman are obviously the strongest so idk).

expected the mechanic to be gotten at d3 and not d2; not sure how significantly this affects everything, but yeah
[/hide]

[hide=game thoughts]
overall i think both sk and mafia played quite well. vain did some nice wolfhunting; mafia set up some great not w/w interactions that carried their backs.
town played pretty badly imo. quickhammers on d3 and d4 were kind of dumb and town should never have let them happen. and eod1 was a tragedy. make a wagon at eod! it's not hard!
[/hide]
